title_en: Why is this banned in the EU but not in the US?
title_es: ¿Por qué está prohibido en la UE y no en EE. UU.?
hook_en: Did you know your candy might have banned ingredients?
hook_es: ¿Sabías que tus dulces podrían tener ingredientes prohibidos?
key_message: Titanium Dioxide is banned in the EU due to potential genotoxicity but is still used in US candies and food products.
visual_elements: cut to a list of candies with titanium dioxide
_source_regulation_context:
ADDITIVES BANNED IN OTHER COUNTRIES BUT LEGAL IN US:
  • Titanium Dioxide (E171): Candy, coffee creamer, frosting, supplements — Banned in: EU (banned 2022) — Health concern: Potential genotoxicity — can damage DNA per EFSA assessment

US OBESITY RATES OVER TIME:
  • 1960: 13.4% (adults), 5.0% (children)
  • 1980: 15.0% (adults), 5.5% (children)
  • 1994: 22.5% (adults), 10.0% (children)
  • 2000: 30.5% (adults), 13.9% (children)
  • 2010: 35.7% (adults), 16.9% (children)
  • 2016: 39.8% (adults), 18.5% (children)
  • 2020: 41.9% (adults), 19.7% (children)
  • 2024: 43.0% (adults), 21.0% (children)

title_es: El problema creciente de América: Obesidad y desregulación
hook_es: ¿Por qué ha aumentado la obesidad en América?
key_message: The rise in obesity rates correlates with deregulation in food additives and lax policies compared to other countries.
visual_elements: animated line graph of obesity rates over time
split screen: healthy meal vs. processed snack
overlay text with obesity statistics from 1960 to 2024
footage of grocery store aisles with processed foods
_source_regulation_context:
